Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal - The Nation Newspaper Monday, March 16, 2026 Advertise With Us E-paper. March 16, 2026 by  Chikodi Okereocha. # Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal. Manufacturers have welcomed Chinese President Xi Jinping’s decision to remove tariffs on imports from 53 African countries starting May 1, 2026, noting that the zero-tariff policy will open one of the world’s largest consumer markets to African producers and manufacturers. In the short term, resource-rich countries may see faster growth in shipments of oil, minerals, and agricultural commodities. “The zero-tariff window should serve as a catalyst for industrial upgrading, regional value chains, and stronger export competitiveness,” it said. Instead, these gains will depend on Africa’s ability to move up the value chain, producing semi-processed and finished goods that meet international quality standards. * ‘What China’s Xi told me about Taiwan’. * What Nigeria can learn from the China’s poverty eradication model. ##### Nigerian manufacturers, others to save $1.4b yearly on Chinese tariff removal 1 minute ago. https://thenationonlineng.net/nigerian-manufacturers-others-to-save-1-4b-yearly-on-chinese-tariff-removal/amp/

What Is Potash Used For? 7 Powerful Benefits For Crops 2026 - Farmonaut Advances in satellite and AI analysis like those used by **Farmonaut** are revolutionizing how potash-bearing minerals are detected and mapped, enabling rapid, non-invasive exploration of new K resources worldwide—crucial for ongoing fertilizer supply in global food systems. Smart potash management is central to future-proofing agriculture: it contributes to **yield stability, crop quality, sustainable soil health, and food security in 2026 and beyond**. A: Potash is primarily used as a fertilizer, supplying the essential nutrient potassium (K) to support crop water regulation, disease resistance, stress tolerance, and overall yield and quality improvement. A: All crops require potassium, but cereals (wheat, maize, rice), tubers (potato, sweet potato), sugar crops (beet, cane), fruit and vegetables, forages, and perennial orchards show especially high demand and yield/quality response to adequate potash supply. **Potash is used for far more than just “feeding plants”—it’s the backbone of resilient, high-output, climate-adaptive agriculture.** As we advance toward 2026, the capacity of **potassium** to regulate growth, boost yield, enhance product quality, and cushion crops against extreme weather becomes ever more central to food system stability. https://farmonaut.com/mining/what-is-potash-used-for-7-powerful-benefits-for-crops-2026
